title: Eustephia cyrtanthoides
taxonomy: Eustephia cyrtanthoides is classified within the family Amaryllidaceae, which consists of flowering plants commonly known as the lily family. This classification places Eustephia cyrtanthoides alongside other closely related species. The genus Eustephia is further grouped within the subfamily Allioideae, which contains species with onion-like characteristics. Within the Allioideae subfamily, Eustephia cyrtanthoides shares similarities with other genera such as Allium and Nothoscordum. This classification system helps to organize and understand the evolutionary relationships among these plant species.

intro: Eustephia cyrtanthoides, commonly known as the "King's Flower," is a flowering plant species belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. Traditional medicine has long recognized the health benefits of Eustephia cyrtanthoides, which include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids like cyrtanthine and lycorine, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. Eustephia cyrtanthoides is typically used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are often used to treat respiratory and dermatological conditions.
